title: Portal Access Application System with ID/Password Login - Frontend Implementation

requirements.summary: Create a public Portal Access Application page at /apply where users can submit applications for portal access by providing their name, email, role, and programme/department
acceptanceCriteria: The page is accessible at `/apply` without authentication.
The form collects Full Name, Email, Role, and Programme/Department.
Submitting the form calls the backend `submitPortalAccessApplication` function.
A success confirmation message is shown on successful submission.
Inline error messages are shown on failure.
The page uses the existing deep navy and gold theme with the university logo visible.

file_operations.description: Create a public-facing portal access application page displaying the university logo (from frontend/public/assets/generated/university-crest.dim_256x256.png) and heading 'Apply for Portal Access'. Render a form with fields: Full Name (text), Email Address (text), Role (dropdown: Student, Staff, Parent, Admin), and Programme/Department (text input with label changing contextually based on selected role: 'Programme' for students, 'Department' for staff/admin, 'Ward's Programme' for parents). On submit, use the useSubmitPortalAccessApplication mutation hook. Display success message 'Your application has been submitted. You will receive your login credentials once your access is approved by the administrator.' on success, or inline error on failure. Style with existing deep navy and gold academic theme.
Add a new public route '/apply' that renders the PortalAccessApplicationPage component without authentication guards.

requirements.summary: Add an Access Applications management section to the Admin Panel where admins can review, approve, or reject pending portal access applications and view generated credentials
acceptanceCriteria: The page is accessible at `/admin/access-applications` and is wrapped with RoleGuard requiring admin role.
All submitted applications are listed in a table with Name, Email, Role, Programme, Date, Status columns.
Pending applications show 'Approve' and 'Reject' action buttons.
Approving an application displays the generated Role ID and Password in a prominent modal or toast.
Rejecting an application updates the status to 'Rejected' immediately in the list.
The Admin sidebar includes a link labelled 'Access Applications' pointing to this page.
React Query list is invalidated and refetched after approve/reject actions.

file_operations.description: Create an admin page wrapped with RoleGuard (admin role) that fetches all portal access applications via useGetPortalAccessApplications query hook. Display applications in a table with columns: Applicant Name, Email, Role, Programme/Department, Submitted Date (formatted from timestamp), Status (rendered as badge with color coding: yellow for pending, green for approved, red for rejected), and Actions column. For pending applications, render 'Approve' and 'Reject' buttons. Clicking Approve calls useApprovePortalAccessApplication mutation and on success displays a modal/dialog showing the generated Role ID and Password (e.g., 'Student ID: MATRIC/2024/0001 | Password: Xk7#mP2q') for the admin to communicate to the applicant. Clicking Reject calls useRejectPortalAccessApplication mutation. Both mutations invalidate the applications query on success to trigger refetch. Include admin sidebar navigation.

Add a 'Access Applications' link to the admin sidebar navigation that routes to '/admin/access-applications'. Position it logically among other admin management links (e.g., below Student Management or Staff Management).
Add a new admin route '/admin/access-applications' that renders the AccessApplicationsPage component wrapped with appropriate guards.

requirements.summary: Create a dedicated ID/Password login page at /id-login where users can authenticate using their role-specific ID (matriculation number, staff ID, parent ID, or admin ID) and password
acceptanceCriteria: The page is accessible at `/id-login` without authentication.
The university logo and name are displayed prominently at the top of the login form.
A Role selector (Student, Staff, Admin, Parent) is present and updates the ID field placeholder text accordingly.
Submitting the form calls `loginWithIdAndPassword` with the entered ID and password.
On success, the user is redirected to their role dashboard (`/student`, `/staff`, `/admin`, or `/parent`).
On failure, an inline error message 'Invalid ID or password. Please check your credentials or contact the administrator.' is shown.
The page is styled with the deep navy and gold academic theme and is mobile responsive.

file_operations.description: Create a public login page at /id-login displaying the university logo (from frontend/public/assets/generated/university-crest.dim_256x256.png), university name, and a login form. Include a Role selector dropdown (Student, Staff, Admin, Parent) that dynamically updates the ID input field placeholder text: 'Enter Matriculation Number' for students, 'Enter Staff ID' for staff, 'Enter Parent ID' for parents, 'Enter Admin ID' for admins. Include a Password input field. On form submission, call useLoginWithIdAndPassword mutation hook with roleId and password. On success, store the returned UserProfile in React Query state (via saveCallerUserProfile or similar mechanism) and redirect to the role-specific dashboard: /student for student role, /staff for staff role, /admin for admin role, /parent for parent role. On failure, display inline error message: 'Invalid ID or password. Please check your credentials or contact the administrator.' Style with deep navy and gold academic theme, ensure mobile responsiveness.
Add a new public route '/id-login' that renders the IdPasswordLoginPage component without authentication guards.

requirements.summary: Add navigation links to 'Apply for Portal Access' and 'ID/Password Login' on the public homepage, portal selection page, and main navigation to guide users to the new application and login flows
acceptanceCriteria: The public homepage displays a banner or card section with a button linking to `/apply`.
The homepage also shows a secondary link to `/id-login` for users who already have credentials.
The Portal Selection page at `/portal` shows links to both `/apply` and `/id-login` beneath the four role cards.
The top navigation bar shows 'Apply for Access' and 'ID Login' links to unauthenticated visitors.

file_operations.description: Add a visible call-to-action banner or card section titled 'New User? Apply for Portal Access' with a primary button linking to '/apply' and a secondary link/button 'Already have credentials? Login with your ID' linking to '/id-login'. Position this section prominently on the homepage (e.g., after the hero banner or stats section). Style consistently with the existing deep navy and gold academic theme.

Add a footer row beneath the four portal selection cards with two prominent links: 'Apply for Portal Access' linking to '/apply' and 'Login with ID & Password' linking to '/id-login'. Style these links as buttons or inline links with appropriate spacing and alignment.

Add 'Apply for Access' link (pointing to '/apply') and 'ID Login' link (pointing to '/id-login') to the public/unauthenticated navigation links section. Position these links logically among other public links (e.g., Home, Admissions, Portal). Ensure they are only visible when the user is not authenticated.

requirements.summary: Add React Query hooks for all portal access application backend functions to enable frontend interaction with the new backend capabilities
acceptanceCriteria: `useSubmitPortalAccessApplication` mutation hook exists and calls the backend function correctly.
`useGetPortalAccessApplications` query hook exists, uses the authenticated actor, and is usable on the admin Access Applications page.
`useApprovePortalAccessApplication` and `useRejectPortalAccessApplication` mutation hooks exist, use the authenticated actor, and invalidate the applications list query on success.
`useGetMyPortalAccessApplication` query hook exists and uses the anonymous actor.
`useLoginWithIdAndPassword` mutation hook exists and uses the anonymous actor.
All hooks follow existing naming conventions and patterns in `useQueries.ts`.

file_operations.description: Add six new React Query hooks following existing patterns in the file: 1) useSubmitPortalAccessApplication (mutation, anonymous actor, accepts name, email, role, programmeOrDepartment parameters, calls backend submitPortalAccessApplication), 2) useGetPortalAccessApplications (query, authenticated actor, calls backend getPortalAccessApplications, query key ['portalAccessApplications']), 3) useApprovePortalAccessApplication (mutation, authenticated actor, accepts appId parameter, calls backend approvePortalAccessApplication, invalidates ['portalAccessApplications'] query on success), 4) useRejectPortalAccessApplication (mutation, authenticated actor, accepts appId parameter, calls backend rejectPortalAccessApplication, invalidates ['portalAccessApplications'] query on success), 5) useGetMyPortalAccessApplication (query, anonymous actor, accepts email parameter, calls backend getMyPortalAccessApplication, query key ['myPortalAccessApplication', email]), 6) useLoginWithIdAndPassword (mutation, anonymous actor, accepts roleId and password parameters, calls backend loginWithIdAndPassword). Ensure proper TypeScript types using backend interface types from backend.d.ts.
